description: Build production code + unit tests from spec

tasks.name: Project scaffolding
tasks.description:
Create the project directory structure and configuration files.

1. Create all directories:
   - lambdas/shared/python/shared/
   - lambdas/discovery/prompts/
   - lambdas/research/prompts/
   - lambdas/script/prompts/
   - lambdas/producer/prompts/
   - lambdas/cover_art/prompts/
   - lambdas/tts/
   - lambdas/post_production/
   - lambdas/site/templates/
   - lambdas/mcp/tools/
   - layers/ffmpeg/
   - sql/
   - terraform/
   - tests/unit/test_shared/
   - tests/unit/test_mcp/
   - tests/integration/
   - tests/e2e/
   - .github/workflows/

2. Create pyproject.toml with configuration for:
   - [tool.pytest.ini_options]: testpaths=["tests"], markers for integration and e2e
   - [tool.mypy]: strict=true, plugins=[], exclude patterns for build dirs
   - [tool.ruff]: line-length=100, target-version="py312"
   - [tool.ruff.lint]: select=["E","F","I","W"], isort with known-first-party=["lambdas","shared","tests"]
   - [tool.ruff.format]: quote-style="double"
   Read docs/spec/ci-pipeline.md for exact configuration.

3. Create .gitignore per docs/spec/packaging-and-deployment.md:
   - build/, *.zip, __pycache__/, .pytest_cache/, .mypy_cache/, *.egg-info/
   - .env, .terraform/, terraform.tfstate*, .terraform.lock.hcl
   - ralph.log, ralph-status.txt, ralph.pid, iterations/

4. Create empty __init__.py files in: tests/, tests/unit/, tests/unit/test_shared/, tests/unit/test_mcp/, tests/integration/, tests/e2e/

tasks.name: Shared types module
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/shared/python/shared/types.py with all TypedDict definitions from the 'Shared Type Definitions' section of type-checking.md.

Required TypedDicts: PipelineMetadata, PipelineState, DiscoveryOutput, ResearchOutput, NotableRepo, ScriptOutput, ScriptSegment, ProducerOutput, CoverArtOutput, TTSOutput, PostProductionOutput.

Use typing.TypedDict. Use 'from __future__ import annotations' for forward references. Match every field name, type annotation, and NotRequired marker exactly from the spec.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from shared.types import PipelineState, DiscoveryOutput, ResearchOutput, ScriptOutput, ProducerOutput, CoverArtOutput, TTSOutput, PostProductionOutput'

tasks.name: Shared observability modules
tasks.description:
Create the three Powertools factory modules:

1. lambdas/shared/python/shared/logging.py — Exports get_logger(service: str) -> Logger. Uses aws_lambda_powertools.Logger with log_level from LOG_LEVEL env var (default INFO). Sets correlation_id_path for X-Ray.

2. lambdas/shared/python/shared/tracing.py — Exports get_tracer(service: str) -> Tracer. Uses aws_lambda_powertools.Tracer with auto_patch=True.

3. lambdas/shared/python/shared/metrics.py — Exports get_metrics(service: str, namespace: str = 'ZeroStars') -> Metrics. Uses aws_lambda_powertools.Metrics.

Read instrumentation.md for the exact patterns. Each module should be under 30 lines.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from shared.logging import get_logger; from shared.tracing import get_tracer; from shared.metrics import get_metrics'

tasks.name: Shared Bedrock client
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/shared/python/shared/bedrock.py — the Bedrock Claude API wrapper.

Two main functions:
1. invoke_model(user_message, system_prompt, model_id, max_tokens, effort) -> str — Single-turn. Calls bedrock-runtime invoke_model. Handles ThrottlingException with exponential backoff (3 retries, 1s base). Extracts text from response. Supports adaptive thinking via 'thinking' block in the Bedrock API budget_tokens field when effort='high' or 'max'.

2. invoke_with_tools(user_message, system_prompt, tools, tool_executor, model_id, max_tokens, max_turns, effort) -> str — Multi-turn agentic loop. Calls converse API with tool definitions. When model returns tool_use, calls tool_executor(tool_name, tool_input) -> str, sends result back. Loops until model returns text without tool_use, or max_turns reached.

Module-level cached client: _bedrock_client = None, lazy-initialized.
Default model_id: 'us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514'

Read type-checking.md for exact function signatures and external-api-contracts.md for Bedrock API details.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from shared.bedrock import invoke_model, invoke_with_tools'

tasks.name: Shared DB and S3 modules + __init__
tasks.description:
Create three files:

1. lambdas/shared/python/shared/db.py — Postgres helper using psycopg2.
   - get_connection() -> connection — reads DB_CONNECTION_STRING env var, sslmode=require, cached module-level.
   - query(sql: str, params: tuple | None = None) -> list[tuple[Any, ...]] — execute + fetchall, returns list of tuples. Uses cursor context manager.
   - execute(sql: str, params: tuple | None = None) -> int — execute + commit, returns rowcount. For INSERT...RETURNING, use query() instead.

2. lambdas/shared/python/shared/s3.py — S3 helper.
   - Module-level cached _s3_client.
   - upload_bytes(bucket, key, data, content_type) -> None
   - upload_file(bucket, key, filepath, content_type) -> None
   - download_file(bucket, key, local_path) -> None
   - generate_presigned_url(bucket, key, expiry=3600) -> str
   Callers pass bucket explicitly (they read S3_BUCKET from env at the call site).

3. lambdas/shared/python/shared/__init__.py — Package init. Re-export all 7 modules:
   from shared import bedrock, db, s3, logging, tracing, metrics, types

Read type-checking.md for exact signatures and external-api-contracts.md for DB/S3 patterns.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from shared.db import get_connection, query, execute; from shared.s3 import upload_bytes, upload_file, download_file, generate_presigned_url'

tasks.name: Discovery handler + prompt
tasks.description:
Create the Discovery Lambda handler and its system prompt.

1. lambdas/discovery/handler.py — Agentic handler using invoke_with_tools.
   Key internal functions (see type-checking.md for exact signatures):
   - _load_system_prompt() -> str — reads prompts/discovery.md with LAMBDA_TASK_ROOT fallback
   - _get_exa_api_key() -> str — from Secrets Manager, cached module-level
   - _execute_exa_search(tool_input) -> str — calls Exa API via urllib.request
   - _execute_query_postgres(tool_input) -> str — calls shared.db.query()
   - _execute_get_github_repo(tool_input) -> str — calls GitHub API, curated field extraction
   - _execute_tool(tool_name, tool_input) -> str — dispatcher
   - _parse_discovery_output(text) -> DiscoveryOutput — JSON parse with fence stripping, validation, star_count < 10 check
   - lambda_handler(event, context) -> PipelineState — main entry point
   TOOL_DEFINITIONS constant with 3 tools: exa_search, query_postgres, get_github_repo.
   Uses @logger.inject_lambda_context, @tracer.capture_lambda_handler, @metrics.log_metrics decorators.

2. lambdas/discovery/prompts/discovery.md — Read docs/spec/prompt-files.md and find the Discovery Agent section. Transcribe the prompt exactly.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.discovery.handler import lambda_handler, _parse_discovery_output'

tasks.name: Research handler + prompt
tasks.description:
Create the Research Lambda handler and its system prompt.

1. lambdas/research/handler.py — Agentic handler using invoke_with_tools with 5 GitHub API tools.
   Key internal functions (see type-checking.md Research Handler section):
   - _load_system_prompt() -> str
   - _execute_get_github_user(tool_input) -> dict — curated fields: login, name, bio, public_repos, followers, created_at, html_url
   - _execute_get_user_repos(tool_input) -> list[dict] | dict — curated: name, description, stargazers_count, language, html_url, pushed_at, fork
   - _execute_get_repo_details(tool_input) -> dict — curated: name, full_name, description, stargazers_count, forks_count, language, topics, etc.
   - _execute_get_repo_readme(tool_input) -> dict — base64-decode content field, return {content: decoded_text}
   - _execute_search_repositories(tool_input) -> dict — curated: total_count, items with name, full_name, description, stargazers_count, language, html_url
   - _execute_tool(tool_name, tool_input) -> str — dispatcher for all 5 tools
   - _build_user_message(event) -> str — assembles discovery fields into user message
   - _parse_research_output(text) -> ResearchOutput — JSON parse with fence stripping, public_repos_count str-to-int coercion, developer_bio null-to-empty-string coercion, notable_repos validation
   - lambda_handler(event, context) -> PipelineState
   All GitHub tools use urllib.request with User-Agent header. On HTTPError/socket.timeout, return {"error": str(e)}.
   TOOL_DEFINITIONS constant with 5 tools.

2. lambdas/research/prompts/research.md — Read docs/spec/prompt-files.md, find the Research Agent section, transcribe exactly.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.research.handler import lambda_handler, _parse_research_output'

tasks.name: Script handler + prompt
tasks.description:
Create the Script Lambda handler and its system prompt.

1. lambdas/script/handler.py — Single-turn handler (NO tools, uses invoke_model).
   Key internal functions (see type-checking.md Script Handler section):
   - _load_system_prompt() -> str
   - _build_user_message(event) -> str — assembles discovery + research data + optional producer feedback into user message
   - _parse_script_output(text) -> ScriptOutput — parse script text, count characters, extract segments by speaker labels (**Hype:**, **Roast:**, **Phil:**), validate character count < 5000
   - lambda_handler(event, context) -> PipelineState
   Character count target: 4000-4500 chars. Hard limit: 5000 chars (ElevenLabs constraint).

2. lambdas/script/prompts/script.md — Read docs/spec/prompt-files.md, find the Script Agent section, transcribe exactly. Contains persona definitions for Hype, Roast, Phil.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.script.handler import lambda_handler, _parse_script_output'

tasks.name: Producer handler + prompt
tasks.description:
Create the Producer Lambda handler and its system prompt.

1. lambdas/producer/handler.py — Single-turn handler that evaluates script quality.
   Key internal functions (see type-checking.md Producer Handler section):
   - _load_system_prompt() -> str
   - _fetch_benchmark_scripts() -> list[str] — queries episodes table for top scripts, returns [row[0] for row in rows]
   - _build_user_message(event) -> str — includes script text, discovery rationale, research highlights, benchmark scripts
   - _parse_producer_output(text) -> ProducerOutput — JSON parse, verdict must be 'PASS' or 'FAIL', score 1-10
   - lambda_handler(event, context) -> PipelineState — returns updated state with producer output
   Uses invoke_model (not invoke_with_tools). Reads from DB for benchmark scripts.

2. lambdas/producer/prompts/producer.md — Read docs/spec/prompt-files.md, find the Producer Agent section, transcribe exactly.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.producer.handler import lambda_handler, _parse_producer_output'

tasks.name: Cover Art handler + prompt
tasks.description:
Create the Cover Art Lambda handler and its prompt template.

1. lambdas/cover_art/handler.py — Invokes Bedrock Nova Canvas (amazon.nova-canvas-v1:0) for image generation.
   Key internal functions (see type-checking.md Cover Art Handler section):
   - _load_prompt_template() -> str — reads prompts/cover_art.md
   - _build_image_prompt(event) -> str — fills template with repo name, language, cover_art_suggestion from script output
   - _generate_image(prompt: str) -> bytes — calls Nova Canvas via bedrock-runtime invoke_model with imageGenerationConfig (width/height 1024, quality standard, cfgScale 8.0). Creates own boto3 client with 300s read_timeout. Returns decoded PNG bytes from base64 response.
   - lambda_handler(event, context) -> PipelineState — generates image, uploads PNG to S3 at episodes/{execution_id}/cover.png

2. lambdas/cover_art/prompts/cover_art.md — Read docs/spec/prompt-files.md, find the Cover Art section, transcribe exactly.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.cover_art.handler import lambda_handler, _generate_image'

tasks.name: TTS handler
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/tts/handler.py — Text-to-speech via ElevenLabs.

Key internal functions (see type-checking.md TTS Handler section):
- _get_elevenlabs_api_key() -> str — from Secrets Manager, cached module-level
- _parse_dialogue_turns(script_text: str) -> list[dict[str, str]] — splits script by speaker labels (**Hype:**, **Roast:**, **Phil:**), maps to voice IDs: Hype=cjVigY5qzO86Huf0OWal, Roast=JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb, Phil=cgSgspJ2msm6clMCkdW9. Returns list of {text, voice_id} dicts. Strips speaker labels from text. Raises ValueError on unknown speakers.
- _call_elevenlabs(turns: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes — POST to /v1/text-to-dialogue, returns MP3 bytes. Model: eleven_multilingual_v2. Output format: mp3_44100_128.
- lambda_handler(event, context) -> PipelineState — parses script, calls ElevenLabs, uploads MP3 to S3 at episodes/{execution_id}/episode.mp3

Constants: SPEAKER_VOICE_MAP, SPEAKER_PATTERN (regex for **Name:**), ELEVENLABS_ENDPOINT, ELEVENLABS_MODEL_ID.

Read docs/spec/external-api-contracts.md for the ElevenLabs API contract and docs/spec/interface-contracts.md for TTSOutput schema.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.tts.handler import lambda_handler, _parse_dialogue_turns'

tasks.name: Post-Production handler
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/post_production/handler.py — Video assembly + DB persistence.

Key internal functions (see type-checking.md Post-Production Handler section):
- _run_ffmpeg(mp3_path: str, png_path: str, mp4_path: str) -> None — runs ffmpeg subprocess to combine cover art PNG + MP3 into MP4 (1024x1024, -loop 1 on image, -shortest, aac audio). Raises RuntimeError on non-zero exit.
- _insert_episode(...) -> int — INSERT INTO episodes ... RETURNING episode_id using shared.db.query()
- _insert_featured_developer(developer_github, episode_id, featured_date) -> None — INSERT INTO featured_developers using shared.db.execute()
- lambda_handler(event, context) -> PipelineState — downloads MP3+PNG from S3 to /tmp, runs ffmpeg, uploads MP4, inserts DB records, returns PostProductionOutput with s3_mp4_key, episode_id, air_date.

Memory: 1024 MB (ffmpeg-intensive). Uses shared.s3.download_file() for S3 downloads. air_date is today's date in Eastern Time (YYYY-MM-DD).

Read docs/spec/interface-contracts.md for PostProductionOutput schema and docs/spec/database-schema.md for the INSERT column list.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.post_production.handler import lambda_handler'

tasks.name: Site handler + templates
tasks.description:
Create the Site Lambda handler and its Jinja2 templates.

1. lambdas/site/handler.py — Website handler for Lambda Function URL.
   Key internal functions (see type-checking.md Site Handler section):
   - _get_episodes() -> list[dict[str, Any]] — query episodes table ordered by air_date DESC
   - _render_template(template_name: str, **context) -> str — Jinja2 rendering from templates/ dir
   - _build_response(status_code: int, body: str, content_type: str = 'text/html') -> dict — Function URL response format {statusCode, headers, body}
   - lambda_handler(event, context) -> dict — parses Function URL event (rawPath), routes / to index, returns HTML. 404 for unknown paths, 500 on errors.
   Generates presigned URLs for MP3 audio playback. Cover art served via CloudFront /assets/* path.

2. lambdas/site/templates/base.html — Base template with dark theme (inline CSS, no external deps), nav with '0 Stars, 10/10' title, footer.

3. lambdas/site/templates/index.html — Extends base. Lists episodes reverse-chronologically. Each episode: repo name as title, developer name, air date, star count, HTML5 audio player (presigned MP3 URL), cover art image.

Read docs/spec/file-manifest.md for template descriptions and docs/spec/interface-contracts.md for the site handler contract.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python python3 -c 'from lambdas.site.handler import lambda_handler'

tasks.name: Database schema
tasks.description:
Create sql/schema.sql with the DDL for all 3 tables as defined in database-schema.md.

Tables: episodes (with episode_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, air_date DATE, all columns from spec), episode_metrics (with UNIQUE constraint on episode_id + snapshot_date), featured_developers (developer_github as PK).

Use IF NOT EXISTS. Include all column types, defaults, foreign keys, and constraints exactly as specified.

tasks.name: Build scripts
tasks.description:
Create all 5 build scripts as specified in packaging-and-deployment.md.

1. build-all.sh — Top-level orchestrator. Runs all 4 build scripts in parallel (background jobs with wait). Exits non-zero if any fail.

2. lambdas/shared/build.sh — pip install psycopg2-binary and aws-lambda-powertools[tracer] into python/ directory alongside shared source, then zip into build/shared-layer.zip.

3. layers/ffmpeg/build.sh — Downloads static ffmpeg binary from johnvansickle.com, creates bin/ffmpeg structure, zips into layers/ffmpeg/ffmpeg-layer.zip.

4. lambdas/site/build.sh — pip install jinja2 into the site Lambda directory.

5. lambdas/mcp/build.sh — pip install mcp[cli] into the MCP Lambda directory.

All scripts use set -euo pipefail. Use --platform manylinux2014_x86_64 --only-binary=:all: for Lambda-compatible packages. Pin versions per spec.
tasks.validation: cd /workspaces/closed-loop-ai-podcast && bash -n build-all.sh && bash -n lambdas/shared/build.sh && bash -n layers/ffmpeg/build.sh

tasks.name: CI workflow
tasks.description:
Create .github/workflows/ci.yml — GitHub Actions CI pipeline as specified in ci-pipeline.md.

Jobs:
1. lint: ruff check + ruff format --check
2. typecheck: mypy --strict on lambdas/ and tests/
3. test: PYTHONPATH=lambdas/shared/python pytest tests/unit/ -v

Trigger on push to main and pull_request. Use Python 3.12. Install deps: ruff, mypy, pytest, aws-lambda-powertools, psycopg2-binary, boto3-stubs, mcp[cli], jinja2.

Follow exact structure from ci-pipeline.md.

tasks.name: Terraform core files
tasks.description:
Create the foundational Terraform files.

1. terraform/main.tf — AWS provider (us-east-1), terraform backend 'local', data sources for aws_caller_identity and aws_region. Default tags: Project=zerostars, ManagedBy=terraform.

2. terraform/variables.tf — All input variables from terraform-resource-map.md: elevenlabs_api_key (sensitive), exa_api_key (sensitive), db_connection_string (sensitive), domain_name (default 'podcast.ryans-lab.click'), project_prefix (default 'zerostars'), alert_email, mcp_allowed_principal, plus alarm threshold variables.

3. terraform/outputs.tf — Exports: state_machine_arn, site_url, s3_bucket_name, mcp_function_url.

Read terraform-resource-map.md for exact resource definitions.

tasks.name: Terraform app resources
tasks.description:
Create the main application Terraform files.

1. terraform/lambdas.tf — All 8 Lambda functions (7 pipeline + 1 site), each with:
   - archive_file data source for deployment package
   - aws_lambda_function resource (Python 3.12, handler paths, memory, timeout, env vars, layers)
   - aws_iam_role + aws_iam_role_policy for each Lambda
   - aws_cloudwatch_log_group (14-day retention)
   Also: shared Lambda Layer (aws_lambda_layer_version) and ffmpeg Lambda Layer.
   No modules — every Lambda defined inline. Use var.project_prefix for naming.

2. terraform/step-functions.tf — aws_sfn_state_machine with inline ASL via jsonencode(). Read docs/spec/step-functions-asl.md for the complete state machine definition. Include IAM execution role with permission to invoke all 7 pipeline Lambdas.

3. terraform/s3.tf — aws_s3_bucket for episode assets, aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block, bucket policy for CloudFront OAC access.

4. terraform/secrets.tf — aws_secretsmanager_secret + aws_secretsmanager_secret_version for ElevenLabs and Exa API keys.

Read terraform-resource-map.md for all resource details.

tasks.name: Terraform edge + observability
tasks.description:
Create the remaining Terraform files.

1. terraform/site.tf — Site Lambda Function URL, CloudFront distribution with two origins (Function URL for HTML, S3 via OAC for /assets/*), ACM certificate (DNS-validated for podcast.ryans-lab.click), Route53 A record alias to CloudFront.

2. terraform/observability.tf — SNS topic for alerts (email subscription via var.alert_email), CloudWatch alarms: 3 pipeline-level (SFN execution failures, timeout, throttle) + 3 per-Lambda × 9 Lambdas (error rate, p99 duration, throttles) + 2 custom metric alarms. Read observability.md for alarm definitions.

3. terraform/mcp.tf — MCP Lambda function, IAM role/policy (needs Step Functions, Lambda invoke, S3, CloudWatch Logs, RDS, CloudFront permissions), Function URL with AWS_IAM auth, CloudWatch log group.

Read terraform-resource-map.md and observability.md for resource details.

tasks.name: MCP handler
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/handler.py — MCP server Lambda entry point using Streamable HTTP transport.

Key components:
- create_mcp_server() function that creates and configures the MCP Server instance
- Registers all 26 tools from tools/ submodules and 5 resources from resources.py
- lambda_handler(event, context) — handles Function URL events, routes to MCP server
- Uses RESPONSE_STREAM invoke mode for SSE streaming

Read docs/spec/mcp-server.md for the complete MCP server specification including tool registration, transport layer, and resource definitions.

Import tools from: tools.pipeline, tools.agents, tools.observation, tools.data, tools.assets, tools.site
Import resources from: resources

tasks.name: MCP resources
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/resources.py — MCP resource handlers.

5 resources (read mcp-server.md for schemas):
1. zerostars://episodes — list recent episodes
2. zerostars://episodes/{episode_id} — single episode detail
3. zerostars://metrics — episode performance metrics
4. zerostars://pipeline-status — current pipeline execution status
5. zerostars://featured-developers — list of featured developers

Each resource returns JSON. Uses shared.db for Postgres queries. Resource URIs use the zerostars:// scheme.

tasks.name: MCP tools __init__
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/tools/__init__.py — Package init for MCP tools.

Should import and re-export the tool registration functions from each tool module (pipeline, agents, observation, data, assets, site). This allows the handler to do: from tools import register_all_tools or similar.

tasks.name: MCP pipeline tools
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/tools/pipeline.py — Pipeline control tools.

5 tools (read mcp-server.md for exact schemas and behavior):
1. start_pipeline — starts Step Functions execution with optional input overrides
2. stop_pipeline — stops a running execution by ARN
3. get_execution_status — gets status of a specific execution
4. list_executions — lists recent executions with optional status filter
5. retry_from_step — starts new execution with resume_from set to a specific step

Uses boto3 Step Functions client. State machine ARN from STATEMACHINE_ARN env var. Lambda function names follow pattern: zerostars-{agent_name}.

tasks.name: MCP agent invocation tools
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/tools/agents.py — Agent invocation tools.

7 tools, one per pipeline Lambda (read mcp-server.md):
invoke_discovery, invoke_research, invoke_script, invoke_producer, invoke_cover_art, invoke_tts, invoke_post_production

Each tool invokes the corresponding Lambda function synchronously via boto3 Lambda client. Function names: zerostars-{agent_name}. Passes a PipelineState-shaped payload. Returns the Lambda response payload parsed as JSON.

Uses boto3 Lambda client from LAMBDA_CLIENT or creates one.

tasks.name: MCP observation tools
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/tools/observation.py — Observation tools.

3 tools (read mcp-server.md):
1. get_agent_logs — queries CloudWatch Logs for a specific Lambda's recent log events
2. get_execution_history — gets Step Functions execution history events
3. get_pipeline_health — aggregates health metrics across pipeline components (Lambda errors, SFN execution stats)

Uses boto3 CloudWatch Logs client and Step Functions client.

tasks.name: MCP data tools
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/tools/data.py — Data query tools.

6 tools (read mcp-server.md):
1. query_episodes — paginated episode listing with optional filters
2. get_episode_detail — single episode by ID
3. query_metrics — episode performance metrics
4. query_featured_developers — list featured developers
5. run_sql — execute arbitrary read-only SQL (SELECT only, with statement_timeout)
6. upsert_metrics — insert or update episode metrics

Uses shared.db for Postgres queries. run_sql must validate SQL is SELECT-only before executing. Sets statement_timeout on cursor directly.

tasks.name: MCP asset tools
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/tools/assets.py — Asset management tools.

3 tools (read mcp-server.md):
1. get_episode_assets — returns S3 keys and presigned URLs for an episode's MP3, MP4, and cover art
2. list_s3_assets — lists objects in the episodes S3 bucket with optional prefix filter
3. get_presigned_url — generates a presigned URL for any S3 key

Uses shared.s3 for presigned URLs and boto3 S3 client for listing. Bucket name from S3_BUCKET env var.

tasks.name: MCP site tools
tasks.description:
Create lambdas/mcp/tools/site.py — Site management tools.

2 tools (read mcp-server.md):
1. invalidate_cache — creates CloudFront invalidation for specified paths (default: /*)
2. get_site_status — aggregates site health: CloudFront distribution status, ACM certificate status, recent error rates

Uses boto3 CloudFront client and ACM client. Distribution ID from CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID env var.

tasks.name: Test fixtures (conftest)
tasks.description:
Create tests/conftest.py with all shared pytest fixtures.

Read docs/spec/testing.md lines 92-465 (section: 'Shared Fixtures (conftest.py)' through end of Mocking Strategy). The spec contains EXACT Python code for the conftest — transcribe it faithfully.
